income_thresholds.description: No direct income threshold. Targeted at NFSA ration card holders in Delhi who have resided in Delhi for at least 3 years. Covers treatment at Delhi government hospitals only.

exclusion_rules.description: Only residents of Delhi are eligible
Must hold a valid NFSA ration card issued in Delhi. This is MANDATORY.
Must have resided in Delhi for at least 3 years continuously. Proof of 3+ years residence required.
Treatment is covered ONLY at Delhi government hospitals. Private hospital treatment is not covered under this scheme.
Families covered under CGHS, ESIC, or employer-provided insurance are excluded

family_criteria.family_definition: Family as per NFSA ration card issued in Delhi. All members listed on the ration card are eligible for treatment.

covered_procedures: Hospitalization expenses at Delhi govt hospitals only

required_documents.name: NFSA Ration Card (Delhi)
required_documents.notes: Must be valid NFSA ration card issued in Delhi. This is MANDATORY — no alternatives accepted.

required_documents.name: Delhi Residence Proof (3+ years)
required_documents.notes: Must prove at least 3 years of continuous residence in Delhi. Voter ID, utility bills, or rent agreements accepted.

enrollment_channels: Delhi government hospital Medical Social Worker (MSW) office
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Visit a Delhi government hospital when hospitalization is needed
Step 2: Meet the Medical Social Worker (MSW) at the hospital
Step 3: Submit Aadhaar, Delhi NFSA ration card, and 3+ years residence proof
Step 4: MSW verifies NFSA card status and residency duration
Step 5: If eligible, treatment cost up to Rs 1.5 lakh covered under Delhi Arogya Nidhi
Step 6: Treatment must be at Delhi government hospital only
Step 7: No pre-enrollment needed — apply at time of hospitalization with required documents
description_for_embedding: Delhi Arogya Nidhi (DAN) is a Delhi government health fund providing up to Rs 1.5 lakh per family for treatment at Delhi government hospitals. Distinct from Delhi Arogya Kosh which provides Rs 5 lakh. Targeted specifically at NFSA ration card holders who have resided in Delhi for at least 3 years. Treatment is covered ONLY at Delhi government hospitals — private hospitals are not included. Delhi opted out of PM-JAY, so DAN and DAK serve as state alternatives. Enrollment is at the point of hospitalization through the Medical Social Worker. Portal: dgehs.delhi.gov.in.
